metadata.split_role: Expanded red/yellow, blue-staining, red-pored, and caution-lane bolete records.
metadata.notes: Physical data split only. Species record bodies were preserved; each canonical slug appears in exactly one base mushroom file. v4.2.84 updated 25 red/caution bolete records with conservative notes, sources, and explicit remaining review reasons. v4.2.86: optional photo patches are treated as fill-only for existing base-record image fields; direct image fallback hardened for Black Velvet Bolete and Suillus tomentosus where applicable. v4.2.87-r2026-04-30-mushroom-suillus-leccinum-credibility4: carried forward with patch-precedence-safe image handling. v4.2.88: 25-record mushroom credibility/review pass; porcini/Tylopilus/Xerocomus records rechecked and remaining red/caution records improved while preserving true image/local-taxonomy review flags.

review_reason_audit.rule: Normalize explicit review signals into review_reasons/review_note and prefix notes for Needs Review list visibility.

records.culinary_uses: Some two-colored boletes are eaten, but this group is easy to confuse with faster-bluing or more suspect red/yellow boletes. Treat as experienced-level only.

mushroom_profile.summary: Minimal bolete seed entry for Bicolor Bolete. Added in the Michigan audit pass; fuller ID notes, sources, and images still need review.
staining: Pores bruise blue promptly; flesh usually faint/erratic blue
mushroom_profile.season_note: Summer and early fall under hardwoods.
research_notes: Species seed added from the Michigan bolete audit.
Needs image coverage.
Needs source links and fuller field notes.
stem_feature: Red stem with yellow near apex

records.edibility_detail: Confirm red cap and stem, bright yellow pore surface, shallow tube layer, limited/erratic blue staining in flesh, and oak/hardwood setting. Avoid if it blues instantly everywhere or smells off.
records.field_identification: Red cap and mostly red stem, bright yellow pores that bruise blue, shallow tube layer, yellow flesh that usually stains blue only faintly or erratically, under hardwoods/oaks. Compare carefully with Boletus sensibilis and pseudosensibilis.

use_links.label: MushroomExpert: Boletus bicolor
use_links.notes: Red/yellow color pattern, shallow tubes, and blue bruising comparison.

records.edibility_notes: Some two-colored boletes are eaten, but this group is easy to confuse with faster-bluing or more suspect red/yellow boletes.
curation_notes: Minimal species seed. Added from the Michigan bolete audit for the clean app so the bolete section is more complete; details, seasonality, and images still need a later review. Baseline audit pass added lane, seasonality, commonness, food quality, host filtering, and comparison notes.

records.mushroom_card_note: Frequently misidentified oak/hardwood bolete; red-brown cap, yellow pores that blue, and yellow stem flushed red below.
records.field_identification: Red to reddish-brown cap, bright yellow young pores bruising blue, yellow stem flushed red over the lower half, moderate blue bruising, and oak/hardwood association. Compare with bicolor and sensibilis.
